# oke-token Provider

The `oke-token` provider generates short-lived authentication tokens for Oracle Kubernetes Engine (OKE) clusters.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    oke-token = {
      source  = "robo-cap/oke-token"
      version = "1.0.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "oke-token" {
  auth             = "ApiKey"
  tenancy_ocid     = "ocid1.tenancy.oc1..exampleuniqueID"
  user_ocid        = "ocid1.user.oc1..exampleuniqueID"
  fingerprint      = "20:3b:97:13:55:1c:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:11:22:33"
  private_key_path = "~/.oci/oci_api_key.pem"
  region           = "us-ashburn-1"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `auth` (String) Authentication mode. Supported values: `ApiKey`, `SecurityToken`, `InstancePrincipal`, `InstancePrincipalWithCerts`, `ResourcePrincipal`, and `OKEWorkloadIdentity`.
- `config_file_profile` (String) Profile name in `~/.oci/config` when configuration is loaded from the OCI config file.
- `fingerprint` (String) Fingerprint for the API signing key used by `ApiKey` authentication.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-formatted API signing private key contents.
- `private_key_password` (String, Sensitive) Password protecting the private key, when the key is encrypted.
- `private_key_path` (String) Path to the PEM-formatted API signing private key file.
- `region` (String) OCI region, for example `us-ashburn-1`.
- `tenancy_ocid` (String) OCI tenancy OCID used by `ApiKey` and `SecurityToken` authentication.
- `user_ocid` (String) OCI user OCID used by `ApiKey` and `SecurityToken` authentication.
